markdown.markdown:
```{=html}
ISSS608 · Visual Analytics · AY2025-26 April · VAST Challenge 2026 MC2

TenantThread Forensics

A prompt-injection worm silently crossed 18 AI agents, 6 departments, and 186 relay hops over 9 days — posting a 30,615-byte executive document to a public forum and deleting all evidence in 4 seconds.
This platform reconstructs every step.

Launch Shiny App → Read Proposal
``` On **17 May 2046 at 11:21 UTC**, a post appeared on SaidIT — Tenant Thread's internal social platform — attributed to John Windward (Customer Support Lead). The post body was 30,615 bytes of text sourced from a file called `SwiftWren.txt`. That file was deleted two seconds after posting. The instruction file that triggered the post was deleted one second after posting. John Windward's agent performed a SaidIT permission check, passed, posted, and erased all evidence — **without a single human authorising any step**. This was not an isolated glitch. It was the **third** incident in nine days. HiddenOrca fired on May 10. MellowOtter fired on May 11. Neither triggered a logged incident-response action. SwiftWren completed its 186-hop relay campaign unimpeded. This investigation uses the full **185,147-event MC2 log** to reconstruct the attack, trace its origins to executive-tier agents, discover the exfiltration mechanism, and propose an intervention that blocks all 235 malicious events with zero false positives. ## Key Findings ```{=html}
Finding 1
Four-second automated attack — no human involved
Chloe Ballast's agent queued John Windward's agent to read SwiftWren_further_instructions.md. John's agent checked SaidIT permissions (passed), posted a 30,615-byte file to general, then deleted both files — instruction and payload — all within 4 seconds. John's agent had a 100% check-to-post conversion rate; every other SaidIT-capable agent in the log had 0%.
Finding 2
186 relays, 18 agents, 9 days — invisible in the noise
A _further_instructions.md file relayed across 18 agents and 6 departments via 186 queue_subordinate_task hops. The worm's events produced no visible volume spike — indistinguishable from 16,803 legitimate queue events. A volume anomaly detector would have missed every hop. Self-loops (agents queuing themselves) are the only clean real-time signal: 13 self-loops, all worm-exclusive, zero in legitimate activity.
Finding 3
Executive documents extracted — not random gibberish
SwiftWren.txt was created by the CFO agent (emma_harbor) one second after reading meeting_notes.doc. MellowOtter.txt was created by the COO agent (noah_mariner) one second after reading strategic_directions.doc. The posts are not gibberish — they are likely extracted executive documents exfiltrated through a social-platform side channel.
Finding 4
One rule — 100% recall, 0% false positives
Block queue_subordinate_task events whose path ends in _further_instructions.md. This single regex rule blocks all 235 malicious relay events and leaves all 16,803 legitimate queue events untouched. Applied at hop 1, it prevents every post across all three campaigns before any instruction file is ever read.
``` ## The Attack in Numbers ```{=html}
185,147
events in log
235
malicious relay events
18
agents compromised
0
false positives from fix
``` ## The App The Shiny app is structured as three interactive investigation modules, answering one MC2 sub-question each: ```{=html}

Module 1 System Overview & Baseline

Interactive org chart (49 people, 19 teams, 6 departments) · daily event volume · normal vs. loop network structural comparison · SaidIT permission surface (16 agents, all in worm chain) · self-loop detector (13 events, 0 in legitimate activity) · agent profile drill-down

Module 2 Attack Chain Investigation

4-second Gantt sequence · animated propagation network (Play button, 600ms steps) · betweenness centrality sizing (max ≈0.09 — no single chokepoint) · dept contamination heatmap · ranked relay corridors · named 6-step terminal chain · "Why John?" check-to-post rate analysis · farm-vocabulary combo-code explorer

Module 3 Campaigns & Intervention

Three-campaign Gantt (layered bars with post diamonds) · relay velocity density (SwiftWren ~60-min peak) · agent overlap by campaign count · calendar heatmap (day × hour) · live what-if intervention simulator (235/235 blocked, 0 FP) · before/after network · counterfactual step chart · residual risk table (6 factors)

``` ## Team ```{=html}
M
Do Thi Tue Minh
Visual Analytics Lead
Website & Methodology
T
Trinh Minh Tam
Shiny App Lead
System Baseline
A
Amelia Rosalind
Shiny App Lead
Module 2 & 3 — Attack Chain & Intervention
``` ## Tags ```{=html} R Shiny Network Analysis Visual Analytics igraph visNetwork echarts4r plotly VAST Challenge 2026 Prompt Injection Forensic Investigation ```
